title: Rich Crunchy Jamaican Vinegar Stir-fry
description: A rich and crunchy crowd-pleaser dish featuring chopped chive in a flavorful vinegar sauce, perfect for vegetarians and those following a Jain diet.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup soy sauce (or tamari for non-vegan option)
2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar or rice vinegar
Freshly ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Prepare the chives by finely chopping them. Place a non-stick pan over medium-high heat and add the vegan butter or margarine.
Once melted, add the chopped chives to the pan and sauté for about 1 minute until they become slightly softened but still retain their crunchiness.
Add lemon juice, soy sauce (or tamari), apple cider vinegar, salt, and pepper. Stir well to combine all the ingredients together.
Simmer the mixture for an additional 2 minutes so that it reduces slightly and the flavors meld together.
While the chive mixture is simmering, heat some vegetable oil in a separate pan over medium-high heat. Add almonds if you're using them and stir-fry until they turn golden brown, about 1-2 minutes.
Drizzle the heated almond pieces into the chive mixture to combine, ensuring that each piece of almond gets coated with the sauce.
Remove from heat immediately to avoid overcooking and allow the rich flavors to marry within the dish. Taste for any additional salt or lemon juice and adjust as necessary.
Serve hot alongside rice or naan bread.
